title: Research Evidence Board layout direction
summary: Choose the primary axis for claims, sources, contradictions, confidence, and unknowns.

sections.title: Primary skeleton · Confidence columns
sections.summary: Claims move between explicit confidence bands while source and contradiction links remain attached.

regions.title: Evidence controls
regions.detail: Decision relevance remains a filter and label rather than the board axis.

regions.title: Low confidence / unknown
regions.detail: Material unknowns stay visible and cannot look like evidence.

sections.title: Choose the Research Evidence Board axis
sections.summary: Both retain confidence and decision relevance; the choice decides which one controls spatial scanning.

options.label: Confidence columns
options.detail: Place claims by confidence and expose decision relevance as a filter, chip, and linked context.
points: 9/10 · Makes uncertainty and contradiction immediately legible.
A reviewer may need a filter to isolate one decision.

options.label: Decision-relevance grouping
options.detail: Group evidence under the decision it can change and show confidence inside each group.
points: 8/10 · Keeps evidence close to action and intent.
Confidence distribution becomes harder to scan across decisions.
